extraction.one_line_summary: POGAČAR attacked solo on the second ascent of Monte Carpegna, dropped everyone decisively, won by nearly 4 minutes extending GC lead to 1:52.
tactical_patterns: Pogačar isolated Porte on descent with Vingegaard/Lander staying fresh, creating 3v1 dynamic that cost Porte 11 seconds to third.
Simmons waited for Ciccone instead of maximizing KOM lead, UAE exploited by accelerating immediately after Simmons regrouped with peloton.
Lander visibly argued with Vingegaard about attacking rhythm before climb, revealing Jumbo uncertain whether to pace or counter-attack Pogačar.
Quick-Step placed two in break despite no GC interest, unusual resource commitment suggests targeting stage win abandoned mid-race.

rider_signals.POGAČAR Tadej: positive: Unbeaten in 2022, gained 4 minutes on 35km climb after teammates exhausted, descent sketchy but sustainable
rider_signals.PORTE Richie: negative: Lost 11 seconds to Lander in GC due to descent positioning errors, now 4th at unrecoverable deficit
rider_signals.LANDER Gijs: positive: Strongest form in extended period, argued tactically with Vingegaard suggesting confidence to attack independently
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Lost 3:35 on climb, teammates including Senechal abandoned him early revealing Quick-Step prioritized break over supporting GC
rider_signals.SIMMONS Quinn: neutral: Waited for Ciccone sacrificing KOM buffer, suggests Israel team orders prioritized stage help over jersey defense
